Assembly: The assembly ddLepDidy1.1 is based on 321x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with HiCanu, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The order and orientation of contigs along SUPER_1 between 17 Mb and 22.6 Mb is uncertain.

Assembly: The assembly ilAnaCroc1.1 is based on 47x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z was assigned by alignment to GCA_947562085.1.

Assembly: The assembly ddAraThal4.1 is based on 79x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled with MBG from PacBio HiFi reads mapping to related genomes. A representative circular sequence was selected for each from the graph based on read coverage. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds are named by synteny based on Arabidopsis thaliana (thale cress) GCA_000001735.2.

Assembly: The assembly ddDesSoph1.hap1.1 is based on 90x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome appears to be a degenerated tetraploid. As a result each pf the chromosomes has HiC signal that suggests it is an old copy of another. For example Chromsomes 1 and 2.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Mollusca; Gastropoda; Heterobranchia; Euthyneura; Nudipleura; Nudibranchia; Doridina; Eudoridoidea; Chromodorididae; Felimare
Assembly: The assembly of Felimare picta (xgFelPict1) is based on 28X ONT data and 29X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Nextdenovo, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 5 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 450 Mb (with the largest being 143 kb). Additionally, 605 regions totaling 92 Mb (with the largest being 2 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 5 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 890 kb (with the largest being 385 kb ). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly drComPalu1.1 is based on 35x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly drComPalu1.1 is based on 35x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Haplotype 2 chromosomes are named after haplotype 1.

Assembly: The assembly icScaQuad2.1 is based on 46x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The order and orientation of scaffolds on Chromosome 1 between ~113.46-118.80Mb, Chromosome 2 between ~64.25-65.96Mb, Chromosome 3 between ~97.82-101.18Mb, Chromosome 5 between ~35.30-36.00Mb and Chromosome X between 0-6.15Mb and 46.87-53.69Mb is unsure. Chromosomes X and Y were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly.

Assembly: The assembly ilLozFors1.hap1.1 is based on 52x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. In addition to the expected Z chromosome, which was identified through BUSCO gene painting aligned with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4), we have detected a triploid set of chromosome 29. Analysis of read coverage indicates that these chromosomes are genuine rather than artefacts resulting from assembly errors. However, to confirm their authenticity and better understand their origin and significance, additional sequencing of both male and female samples will be necessary.

Assembly: The assembly mOrcOrc1.1 is based on 34x PacBio data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/) and Hi-C data made publicly available by DNA Zoo (https://dnazoo.org); PMID:28336562. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Order and orientation of scaffolds in a repetitive region of chromosome 17 (~33.6Mb) is uncertain.
